title: IPC Methods Overview and exec() Role
description: A flowchart showing how a parent process uses fork() and exec() to create a new program, and the subsequent decision point for IPC method selection based on the need for communication.
elements: IPC Methods: Pipes, FIFOs, Message Queues, Shared Memory, Semaphores, Signals, Sockets
relationships: Parent calls fork() to create a Child
Child calls an exec() function to replace its image with a New Program
After exec(), the New Program and Parent must choose an IPC Method to communicate
